mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate signals robust aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with above-trend GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment levels triggers risk-off repricing as investors anticipate falling consumer spending and corporate revenue, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates a synchronized withdrawal of household balance sheet leverage, which suppresses durable goods demand and equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 200 basis points annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising policy rates combined with accelerating headline inflation compress real yields and increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns during active tightening phases.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in identified tightening periods are not statistically lower than returns in neutral or easing periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects resilient manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO prints are not significantly higher than the median return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the civilian unemployment rate signals weakening labor market conditions and rising wage rigidity, prompting investors to reduce equity exposure and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 45% over any 2-year period, or 60-day forward returns in rising unemployment regimes are not significantly negative relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in consumer prices and federal funds rates indicates a stagflationary feedback loop where margin compression and higher discount rates jointly depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect fails to explain incremental variance in 20-day forward returns beyond the sum of individual series effects (F-test p > 0.05), or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the civilian unemployment rate signals weakening labor market conditions and rising wage rigidity, prompting investors to reduce equity exposure and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon during post-shock recessions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following the specified divergence pattern are not significantly negative compared to non-divergence periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX levels.
